texto: The Four Stages of Writer’s BlockW.B. Stage I: I want to write but I can’t.W.B. Stage II: I have to write but I can’t. W.B. Stage I: I don’t want to write but I have to.W.B. Stage I: I don’t have time for writing … and, honestly, I don’t feel like writing.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Katerina Stoykova Klemer: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “The Four Stages of Writer’s BlockW.B. Stage I: I want to write but I can’t.W.B. Stage II: I have …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, writer-s-block, writing pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de writing e writing-life.
